name: Arvell Shaw
abstract: Arvell Shaw (September 15, 1923 – December 5, 2002) was an American jazz double-bassist, best known for his work with Louis Armstrong.

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: the bass players fluctuated, either Arvell Shaw or Benny Moten, different bass players. But with Charlie Shavers and Coleman Hawkins.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Louie had, quintets, with Earl Hines, Jack Teagarden, Barney Bigard, Arvell Shaw, Louie and me.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: This was Vic Dickenson, myself, Arvell Shaw, Kansas Fields, George Wein on the piano.
